title: Hooks, Quality Gates, Signing, and Repository Security · Collaboration Transfer
summary: Add useful local feedback without pretending client-side hooks enforce server policy or executing untrusted repository code silently. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a bounded Git automation and trust policy with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
Keep hook logic fast, deterministic, documented, opt-in where appropriate, and delegated to versioned scripts.
Separate local convenience hooks, CI checks, protected branches, reviews, and server-side enforcement by authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A cloned repository proposes a pre-commit hook, diff driver, and build script. Determine what Git runs automatically, what is bypassable, and which server or CI gate owns enforcement before trust.

What must be established before implementation?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for map client and server hook names to operations, inputs, environment, bypass behavior, and failure effects.
